steps.title: Pin down the claim
steps.content: Martí argues that a society's spiritual well-being depends on the character of its literary culture. The supporting quote must directly assert that literature is vital to a people's inner life or spirit.

steps.title: Match a quote that ties literature to spiritual vitality
steps.content: Choice A says poetry "fortifies or brings anguish," "shores up or demolishes souls," and "gives them the desire and strength for life" — literature is cast as more necessary than industry because it sustains the spirit. That is exactly the claim.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'more necessary to a people than industry itself': directly links literature to a society's spiritual strength and well-being. ✓
  • Choice B — 'history of the nations can be told': makes literature a historical record, not a source of spiritual well-being — misses the claim. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Where will a race of men go': laments loss of faith and thinking but does not say a society's spirit depends on the character of its literary culture. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Listen to Walt Whitman': praises one poet's personal virtues; does not state that society's spiritual well-being depends on its literature. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
